headline: Universal's 'Super Mario Galaxy Movie' Secures Top Position at China Box Office
deck: The animated feature commenced its theatrical run by leading the national box office during the April 3–5 weekend, achieving a notable debut amidst a competitive film market.
body: Universal Pictures' 'The Super Mario Galaxy Movie' launched into the premier position at the China box office, marking a significant entry into one of the world's largest cinematic markets. The film ascended to the top spot during the three-day period spanning April 3 to April 5.

The animated production garnered RMB57.1 million, an approximate equivalent of $8.3 million, in its inaugural frame. This performance established the film as the leading draw, positioning it ahead of both domestic and international releases during the specified weekend.

Concurrently, China Film's drama 'It’s OK' advanced to second place in its second weekend of exhibition. The domestic feature accumulated an additional $6.1 million in weekend receipts, elevating its cumulative box office total to $9.9 million.

Following closely, Columbia Pictures’ science-fiction epic 'Project Hail Mary' secured the third position in the market. The film added $5.7 million to its earnings during the same period, bringing its total gross revenue to $27.5 million.
The weekend's new releases also saw Dirty Monkeys’ 'Now I Met Her' debut in fourth place. The film commenced its theatrical run by earning $4.1 million.

The varied performances of these films underscore the dynamic and competitive nature of the Chinese cinematic landscape, where both major international releases and domestic productions vie for audience engagement.
